answer: Xero uses OAuth 2.0 for API authentication [1][2]. The standard method is the authorization code flow, where your application redirects users to Xero to authorize access, then exchanges a temporary authorization code for an access token set [3][4]. For machine-to-machine integrations to a single organization, Xero offers Custom Connections, which use the OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant to obtain access tokens without user interaction [3][4]. All API requests must include a valid bearer access token in the header [2][3]. Access tokens expire after 30 minutes and can be refreshed using a stored refresh token [3][4].
